name: Almond-Raspberry Thumbprint Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter and sugar in a medium bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
2. Add almond filling, egg yolks, and almond extract; beat until blended.
3. Stir flour, baking powder, and salt into the mixture with a wooden spoon to form a soft dough.
4. Cover and refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Shape dough into balls about 1-inch in diameter.
7. Place the balls on ungreased baking sheets, spacing them about 1-1/2 inches apart.
8. Press a thumb into the center of each ball to create an indentation.
9. Spoon half a teaspoon of raspberry filling into each indentation.
10. Bake for 11-13 minutes or until the edges of the cookies are golden brown.
11. Allow cookies to cool on baking sheets for 1 minute before transferring them to wire racks to cool completely.
Makes about 5 dozen cookies
